raw_response: {"rating": -0.3, "lean": "Center", "explanation": "This is a news report summarizing Gallup survey results on attitudes toward the criminal justice system. The author’s prose is largely neutral and data-driven, presenting partisan and racial breakdowns without evaluative judgment. A mild left-lean appears in an editorial header and the closing emphasis on social/economic solutions as most effective, but overall framing remains balanced, yielding a Center rating. Detailed Analysis: (1) The piece relies on percentages, timelines, and subgroup breakdowns with neutral verbs (e.g., “finds,” “mark,” “are divided”) and avoids value-laden descriptors, indicating minimal ideological framing; (2) Partisan perspectives are presented symmetrically (Republicans favor tougher approaches; Democrats are more divided and more likely to see unfairness), with no moral judgments or loaded language toward either side; (3) The section header asserting law-and-order measures are “not a cure-all” and the conclusion that social/economic solutions are “key” introduce slight left-coded framing that prioritizes root-cause remedies; (4) Counterbalancing right-coded framing is also present: the story highlights and early paragraphs foreground that a majority say the system is “not tough enough” and that more favor strengthening law and order than reducing bias, without critique or downplaying. As these patterns offset, the net lean is minimal and centered."}

parsed_output.explanation: This is a news report summarizing Gallup survey results on attitudes toward the criminal justice system. The author’s prose is largely neutral and data-driven, presenting partisan and racial breakdowns without evaluative judgment. A mild left-lean appears in an editorial header and the closing emphasis on social/economic solutions as most effective, but overall framing remains balanced, yielding a Center rating. Detailed Analysis: (1) The piece relies on percentages, timelines, and subgroup breakdowns with neutral verbs (e.g., “finds,” “mark,” “are divided”) and avoids value-laden descriptors, indicating minimal ideological framing; (2) Partisan perspectives are presented symmetrically (Republicans favor tougher approaches; Democrats are more divided and more likely to see unfairness), with no moral judgments or loaded language toward either side; (3) The section header asserting law-and-order measures are “not a cure-all” and the conclusion that social/economic solutions are “key” introduce slight left-coded framing that prioritizes root-cause remedies; (4) Counterbalancing right-coded framing is also present: the story highlights and early paragraphs foreground that a majority say the system is “not tough enough” and that more favor strengthening law and order than reducing bias, without critique or downplaying. As these patterns offset, the net lean is minimal and centered.

explanation: This is a news report summarizing Gallup survey results on attitudes toward the criminal justice system. The author’s prose is largely neutral and data-driven, presenting partisan and racial breakdowns without evaluative judgment. A mild left-lean appears in an editorial header and the closing emphasis on social/economic solutions as most effective, but overall framing remains balanced, yielding a Center rating. Detailed Analysis: (1) The piece relies on percentages, timelines, and subgroup breakdowns with neutral verbs (e.g., “finds,” “mark,” “are divided”) and avoids value-laden descriptors, indicating minimal ideological framing; (2) Partisan perspectives are presented symmetrically (Republicans favor tougher approaches; Democrats are more divided and more likely to see unfairness), with no moral judgments or loaded language toward either side; (3) The section header asserting law-and-order measures are “not a cure-all” and the conclusion that social/economic solutions are “key” introduce slight left-coded framing that prioritizes root-cause remedies; (4) Counterbalancing right-coded framing is also present: the story highlights and early paragraphs foreground that a majority say the system is “not tough enough” and that more favor strengthening law and order than reducing bias, without critique or downplaying. As these patterns offset, the net lean is minimal and centered.
